danger_signals.visual_form: Aliens fly into formation from top and sides of screen, then dive toward player
Bullets traveling downward from enemy positions
danger_signals.meaning: Direct damage hazard; collision causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Large circular beam energy connecting Boss Galaga to player ship
danger_signals.meaning: Ship capture mechanic; loss of player control and life

danger_signals.visual_form: Four large aliens positioned at top of enemy formation, distinct visual size
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat entity; requires two shots to destroy, can capture ship

danger_signals.visual_form: Player ship sprite overlap with enemy sprite
Higher density of projectiles, faster dive patterns
danger_signals.meaning: World state escalation; danger intensifies as game progresses

safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage for player movement and shooting

safety_signals.visual_form: Aliens fly in preset formation without firing
Two connected ship sprites moving as single unit with expanded firepower visual
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced player state; increased combat capability and protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Player ship at bottom of screen, not colliding with threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien creature design; Boss Galaga as elite unit variant
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard aliens vs. Boss Galaga (larger, stronger); morphing enemies with alternate attack patterns including Galaxian Flagship
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Formation positioning; alien sprites occupy upper screen area as invading force
Player position at bottom screen boundary; defensive posture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage emblems displayed at bottom-right of screen; enemy formation transitions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become more aggressive per stage; increased projectile frequency and dive speed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player ship appears integrated into enemy formation at top of screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Captured ship becomes part of enemy formation; player loses direct control until rescue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescued ship visually attaches to player's remaining ship; dual-fighter sprite appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dual-fighter fires with additional firepower; larger hitbox; enhanced combat capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescued ship remains in formation, morphs to enemy appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Former player ship acts as hostile alien; will attack player in future stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aliens fly in preset, non-threatening pattern
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemy projectiles fired; player can safely harvest points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage counter resets to zero; visual glitches possible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game may reset, play levels incorrectly, lock up, or shift difficulty based on dip switch settings

environmental_storytelling.example: Boss Galaga positioned at apex of enemy formation; visual size and positioning establish command structure
Emblems at bottom-right indicate stage number; visual communication of temporal progression through war campaign
Some enemies transform into new types with different attack patterns (Galaxian Flagship); communicates enemy adaptation and increasing sophistication
Player's own ship can turn against them if rescue fails; visual irony of friendly becoming foe
Increased projectile density and faster dive speeds create visual cacophony indicating world-state danger increase

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves toward player; fires projectiles; collision damages player
Positioned at top; may activate tractor beam toward player
Pulls player ship upward into formation; requires two shots to destroy
Vulnerable to interception; if destroyed during dive, releases captured ship for rescue; if destroyed in formation, captured ship becomes enemy
Changes visual form and attack pattern; increases unpredictability
Moves laterally at bottom; fires upward; has single hitbox
Moves as dual unit; fires with enhanced firepower; larger hitbox increases collision risk but improves damage output
Integrates into enemy formation; behaves as hostile alien in subsequent stages

notes: Galaga uses minimal visual palette and sprite work typical of early-1980s arcade hardware, yet communicates complex world-state through positional hierarchy, formation patterns, and behavioral transformations. The tractor beam mechanic creates a unique danger signal—the visual beam itself becomes a manifestation of threat and player vulnerability. Enemy morphing and ship transformation mechanics demonstrate how limited sprite animation can convey escalating complexity. Stage emblems provide explicit world-state indexing. The 'kill screen' at stage 255 represents a systemic boundary manifested through cascading visual/behavioral glitches. No explicit faction color-coding or environmental markers exist; faction identity relies entirely on sprite design and positioning paradigms.
